title: gagea bohemica subsp. gallica
intro: Gagea bohemica subsp. gallica, also known as the yellow gagea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family. It has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in European folk remedies, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Gagea bohemica subsp. gallica include infusions and decoctions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections.
